affected_layers: UI / Presentation (MountainCondor.astro — new component replacing MountainHiker.astro)
Client-side scripting (vanilla JS scroll listener + rAF loop — updated geometry constants)
problem_statement: The existing scroll-driven animation uses a human hiker silhouette. The brand (Aconcagua / Andes) is better represented by the Andean condor, South America's largest soaring bird and an iconic symbol of the region. Replacing the hiker with a condor in flight strengthens brand identity and shifts the animation metaphor from effort-on-foot to freedom-in-flight.
solution_strategy: Create MountainCondor.astro with an inline condor SVG, soaring/gliding CSS keyframes, and the same scroll-driven rAF positioning loop. Update Hero.astro import and tag. Delete MountainHiker.astro to prevent dead code.
entry_point: Automatic — condor appears at the bottom of the Hero section on page load, above the mountain ridge. Animation activates on scroll. No user action required.
user_story: As a site visitor, I want to see an Andean condor soaring gracefully above the mountain silhouette as I scroll, so that the brand identity (freedom, Andes, South America) is reinforced at the most impactful visual transition on the page.

files_to_change.purpose: New Astro component: condor SVG (viewBox 0 0 60 32, spread wings, white collar, body, tail), scoped CSS (condor-wrapper positioning, .condor absolute, @keyframes condor-glide / wing-l / wing-r, reduced-motion override), <script> block with RIDGE constants, ridgeY() interpolation, HOVER_OFFSET=18px above ridge, lerp rAF loop, scroll/idle state switching
Update import (line ~2) from MountainHiker to MountainCondor, and JSX tag (line ~67) from <MountainHiker /> to <MountainCondor />
Remove obsolete hiker component — replaced entirely by MountainCondor.astro. Prevents dead code.

soaring.keyframes: wing-l / wing-r: ±8° wing-tip oscillation, 0.8s ease-in-out infinite

css_variables_used: var(--accent) — primary condor fill colour (brand gold, adapts to theme)

elements: Body — central ellipse (cx=30, cy=19, rx=7, ry=5)
White collar — ellipse (cx=30, cy=14, rx=4, ry=2.5, fill=var(--white))

positioning_formula.note: Condor belly aligns with ridge point, then HOVER_OFFSET lifts it above the surface. Smoothed via lerp at LERP_FACTOR=0.10 per rAF frame.

reduced_motion.behaviour: Condor placed statically at mountain peak (62% x, 20% y ridge point), no keyframe animations applied

execution_phases.name: Domain & Shared Types
execution_phases.note: Pure UI feature — no shared packages, no backend, no state management

execution_phases.name: SVG Asset Design
steps: RED: Define acceptance criteria for condor silhouette
GREEN: Implement condor SVG in MountainCondor.astro

execution_phases.name: Scroll-Driven Logic
steps: RED: Define expected X/Y positions at 0%, 50%, 100% scroll
GREEN: Implement <script> with updated constants and HOVER_OFFSET
REFACTOR: Tune LERP_FACTOR for graceful flight feel

execution_phases.name: Integration & Exposure
steps: Modify Hero.astro line ~2: change import to MountainCondor

acceptance_criteria: MountainCondor.astro created with condor SVG, CSS animations, and scroll script
Hero.astro import and tag updated to MountainCondor
Condor visible above ridge across full scroll range
Soaring animation during scroll; gliding at idle
prefers-reduced-motion: static condor at mountain peak
No layout shift, no JS errors, no performance regression
aria-hidden and pointer-events: none maintained (purely decorative)
assumptions: The RIDGE array and MOUNTAIN_H constant in Hero.astro clip-path have NOT changed since the previous hiker spec — they must match exactly.
The project uses `var(--accent)` (#c88a3e brand gold) as the primary condor colour, which contrasts well against both the mountain silhouette and the hero background.
No test framework is configured for Astro components — verification is manual + astro check + build.
risks: Condor SVG width (60px) is more than double the hiker width (28px). At very narrow viewports (<375px) the condor may overflow the left edge at X_START=8%. Mitigate: consider clamping X to max(0, xPercent * viewportWidth - CONDOR_W_HALF).
HOVER_OFFSET=18px lifts condor well above the ridge. If the hero-mountain height or clip-path changes, the offset may need recalibration.
Wing animation transform-origin must be set on the <g> wrapper, not the <svg> root, for correct wing-flap geometry.
